Home / Vendors / Securiti.ai
Vendor Profile — Data Security Platform
SC
Securiti.ai
Data Command Center — AI Governance and Privacy Operations
securiti.ai
Deployment
Agentless (API-based)
Coverage
Cloud + SaaS
AI governance
Native — core product focus
Pricing
Enterprise — contact for pricing
Founded
2019
Compare
vs. BigID

Securiti.ai positions itself as a Data Command Center — a platform that blends DSPM, AI governance, privacy operations, and compliance into a single product. The breadth of that positioning is both its strength and the complexity in evaluating it: Securiti does more things than any other platform in the market, and whether that breadth serves your requirements depends on whether you need most of what it covers or only some of it. For privacy and compliance buyers dealing simultaneously with GDPR requirements, AI deployment governance, and DSAR volume, Securiti's consolidated approach is genuinely useful. For buyers with narrower requirements, the depth in specific areas may lag more purpose-built alternatives.

Architecture

Securiti connects to cloud environments and SaaS platforms via API. The platform builds a unified data intelligence layer across connected sources: classification of sensitive data, mapping of data flows, identity and access context, and regulatory metadata. This intelligence layer feeds multiple workflow modules: DSPM, AI governance, DSAR, privacy program management, and consent management.

The AI governance module — which Securiti has invested in aggressively as the AI data security market has emerged — adds visibility into what data AI systems are accessing and ingesting, classification of AI training datasets and model outputs for sensitive data, and governance controls over AI application data access. This is the fastest-evolving part of the Securiti platform as of mid-2026.

Key capabilities

DSPM and data classification. Discovery and classification of sensitive data across cloud and SaaS environments. Coverage for PII, PHI, financial data, and regulatory categories. Classification accuracy and coverage depth are solid for cloud and SaaS environments; on-premises coverage is more limited than BigID's connector library.

AI governance and AI data security. Securiti has made this a primary product focus. The platform scans AI training datasets, monitors what data AI systems access, classifies AI system outputs for sensitive data, and provides controls to restrict what data AI applications can access. For organizations deploying LLMs and AI assistants at enterprise scale, Securiti is among the most developed platforms for governing the data layer underneath those deployments.

DSAR automation. Data Subject Access Request handling: automated discovery of all personal data associated with a subject across connected systems, report generation, and request workflow management. Comparable in function to BigID's DSAR module; the choice between them often comes down to the breadth of data sources each organization needs to search.

Privacy program management. Records of Processing Activities (RoPA), consent management, cookie compliance, privacy impact assessments, and privacy policy management. This is content that security-oriented DSPM platforms do not address and that BigID covers only partially. For privacy program managers rather than security engineers, Securiti's workflow coverage is the most complete available.

Data flows and cross-border transfer mapping. Automated mapping of data flows between systems, including cross-border transfers. Produces the data transfer records required for GDPR Article 30 compliance and data residency audits.

Strengths
  • AI governance capabilities are among the most developed in the market; purpose-built for the AI data security use case
  • Privacy program management workflows (consent, RoPA, PIA) go beyond what any other DSPM platform covers
  • DSAR automation is strong and competitive with BigID for cloud and SaaS-heavy environments
  • Single platform for DSPM, AI governance, and privacy operations reduces tooling sprawl for privacy-led security programs
  • Agentless deployment is fast; no endpoint management
Limitations
  • On-premises and legacy data source coverage is narrower than BigID; cloud and SaaS-centric
  • No behavioral analytics or real-time DDR
  • Platform breadth means some capability areas are shallower than purpose-built alternatives
  • Security-team buyers focused on threat detection will find limited value; the platform is designed for privacy and compliance workflows
  • Evaluation complexity is higher than narrower platforms; requires scoping which modules are relevant before a POC
Who this fits

Securiti is the right platform for organizations that need DSPM, AI governance, and privacy program management from a single vendor, whose data estate is primarily cloud and SaaS, and where the privacy or data governance team is the primary buyer. It is particularly well-suited for organizations deploying AI systems at scale who need to govern what data those systems access, which is the fastest-growing use case in the platform's customer base.

It is not the right fit for organizations with significant on-premises or legacy data estates (BigID is better there), for security teams focused on behavioral threat detection (Varonis), or for cloud-only buyers who need classification depth and shadow data discovery above all else (Cyera). For buyers choosing between Securiti and its closest peer, the BigID vs. Securiti comparison covers the decision directly.

Related: BigID vs. Securiti  ·  BigID profile
